title: QUIC Interop Post-Handshake Stream Open/Accept Work Item
summary: Define and land the smallest child-process prerequisite that proves the managed client opens the first application stream after handshake completion and the server accepts it on the same real harness path without claiming transfer support.
completion_notes: Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
verification_plan: Run the new INT requirement-home test, the existing handshake/public guards, the client 1-RTT readiness proof, and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; then inspect the harness README, plan doc, and gap ledger to confirm the slice stays smaller than transfer and transfer/retry remain `127`.
planned_changes:
- Add the `post-handshake-stream` testcase branch in `InteropHarnessRunner` and keep it on the managed child-process path.
- Sequence the client to open the first outbound application stream after connect completes, and sequence the server to accept the first inbound application stream after accept completes.
- Add requirement-home coverage for the first post-handshake open/accept pair on the real harness path and keep `transfer` and `retry` honest.
- Keep the supported public boundary unchanged and do not add byte-delivery or EOF claims.
